name: Cyclic Glycine-Proline
subtitle: IGF-1 Bioavailability Regulator & Neuroprotective Peptide

molecular.halfLife: Extended stability (more stable than linear GPE precursor)

overview: Endogenous cyclic dipeptide found in plasma, breast milk, and cerebrospinal fluid. Functions as metabolite of IGF-1, regulating its bioavailability through competitive binding to IGFBP-3. Demonstrates neuroprotective, cognitive-enhancing, and cardioprotective properties supported by clinical trials.
mechanism: Competes with IGF-1 for IGFBP-3 binding, normalizing bioavailable IGF-1 levels. Acts as positive allosteric modulator of AMPA and GABA-A receptors. Increases BDNF levels via Akt/MDM2-p53 pathway activation.

items.name: Cognitive Enhancement
items.description: Higher cGP levels correlate with improved memory retention in elderly populations

items.name: Neuroprotection
items.description: Protects neural stem cells from oxidative stress; efficacy demonstrated in stroke models

items.name: Alzheimer's Disease
items.description: Mouse studies show reduced amyloid plaque load and improved spatial memory

items.name: Parkinson's Disease
items.description: Clinical study showed increased CSF cGP and reduced anxiety/depression scores

items.name: Type-2 Diabetes
items.description: Clinical trial improved vascular complications and normalized HbA1c%

items.name: Blood Pressure Regulation
items.description: Demonstrated systolic blood pressure reduction in clinical observations

items.name: Stroke Recovery
items.description: Higher cGP/IGF-1 ratios associated with favorable clinical outcomes

items.name: Healthy Aging
items.description: Maintained ratios correlate with preserved cognitive function with age

deliveryMethods.overview: Preferred route with excellent bioavailability and blood-brain barrier crossing.

interactions.notes: cGP is metabolite of IGF-1; regulates bioavailability through competitive IGFBP-3 binding
Different pathways; BPC-157 promotes tissue repair, cGP modulates IGF-1
Both offer neuroprotection and nootropic effects through distinct pathways
Complementary cognitive effects; Selank addresses anxiety, cGP optimizes IGF-1
Both affect growth hormone/IGF-1 axis; monitor combined effects on levels

timeline.effects: Possible subtle improvements in mental clarity
Enhanced cognitive function and focus may become noticeable
Optimal IGF-1 regulation and sustained cognitive support

contraindications: Consult healthcare provider if taking IGF-1 or growth hormone medications

good: Third-party tested for purity and potency
Clear cGP content labeling (typically in micrograms per serving)
Natural blackcurrant-derived sources from New Zealand
Sealed containers protected from moisture and light
warning: Products lacking clear cGP content specification
bad: Extreme therapeutic claims unsupported by research

references.title: IGF-1 Homeostasis Regulation Study
references.keyFindings: cGP regulates IGF-1 bioavailability through IGFBP-3 competitive binding

references.title: Alzheimer's Mouse Model Study
references.keyFindings: Significantly improved spatial memory; reduced hippocampal plaques 42.3→14.5 per mm²

references.title: Parkinson's Disease Clinical Study
references.keyFindings: Blackcurrant supplementation increased CSF cGP 74%; reduced anxiety/depression

references.title: Stroke Recovery & Cognitive Function Review
references.keyFindings: Higher cGP/IGF-1 ratios associated with favorable stroke outcomes

quickStats.frequency: Once daily, preferably in the morning
quickStats.storage: Room temperature in sealed container, protected from moisture and light
